creator: andrewb@cs.mcgill.ca
text:
I have had no luck with amanda on this new machine. It seems that the estimates do work:

planner: time 5849.901: got result for host host disk /home/Crypto/Labo: 0 -> 48051390K, 1 -> 48051390K, 2 -> 48051400K
planner: time 5849.901: got result for host host disk /dev/mirror/gm0s1a: 0 -> 266992K, 1 -> 267006K, 2 -> 267011K

dmesg on the client shows:

pid 9093 (amandad), uid 2: exited on signal 11 (core dumped)
pid 19762 (amandad), uid 2: exited on signal 11 (core dumped)

The amanda report shows the following:

client using dump:

/-- host       /dev/mirror/gm0s1d lev 1 STRANGE
sendbackup: start [axon:/dev/mirror/gm0s1d level 1]
sendbackup: info BACKUP=/sbin/dump
sendbackup: info RECOVER_CMD=/usr/bin/gzip -dc |/sbin/restore -f - ...
sendbackup: info COMPRESS_SUFFIX=.gz
sendbackup: info end
|   DUMP: Date of this level 1 dump: Wed Dec 13 15:22:35 2006
|   DUMP: Date of last level 0 dump: the epoch
|   DUMP: Dumping snapshot of /dev/mirror/gm0s1d (/usr) to standard output
|   DUMP: mapping (Pass I) [regular files]
|   DUMP: mapping (Pass II) [directories]
|   DUMP: estimated 2490764 tape blocks.
|   DUMP: dumping (Pass III) [directories]
? dumper: strange [missing size line from sendbackup]
? dumper: strange [missing end line from sendbackup]
\--------


Client using tar:

/-- axon       /home/Crypto/Labo lev 0 STRANGE
sendbackup: start [axon:/home/Crypto/Labo level 0]
sendbackup: info BACKUP=/usr/local/bin/gtar
sendbackup: info RECOVER_CMD=/usr/local/bin/gtar -f - ...
sendbackup: info end
? dumper: strange [missing size line from sendbackup]
? dumper: strange [missing end line from sendbackup]
\--------

on the client /tmp/amanda/client:

With dump:

sendbackup: debug 1 pid 19763 ruid 2 euid 2: start at Wed Dec 13 15:22:32 2006
axon# cat /tmp/amanda-report

dmesg on the client shows:

The amanda report shows the following:

sendbackup: version 2.5.1p2
Reading conf file "/usr/local/etc/amanda/amanda-client.conf".

How-To-Repeat: portupgrade -Rf amanda-client
On the backup server: start amdump
